programme.title: Capítulo 178
programme.desc: Nico's parents, Yuli and Joel, confront him about Lupita not being Jewish. However, Joel accepts their love while Yuli does not. Roberta gives Diego the signed Lenny Kravitz guitar. Karen demands her father to fire Nico's dad. Alma covers for Mía by saying Gastón is her boyfriend. The guys give Giovanni and Vico a hard time for dating. Gastón is angry that Mía and Miguel will be going out with Franco again. José reveals that her and Téo are officially dating. Valeria overhears Mía's conversation with Alma. Alma learns that Roberta and Diego are dating. Tomás blurts about the bet.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Capítulo 180
programme.desc: Mía and Miguel go search for answers. Diego realizes he's falling in love with Roberta. José meets with a woman from the orphanage to find out who her tutor is. Pilar gives out cheat sheets in order to get revenge. Alma comes up with a plan to keep Mía from going to Paris. Roberta tells Celina the truth of the date with Simon.

programme.title: Capítulo 181
programme.desc: Miguel tells Mía a plan to find out if Gastón is a millionaire. Roberta learns from Simon and Tomás the truth about Diego's intentions. Mía asks Gastón for money for her birthday party; he agrees to pay for everything at no expense. José and Téo accept their abandonment and put their trust in each other; Gastón interrupts with threats to both. Roberta goes home; Alma slaps Diego for hurting Roberta. Miguel overhears Gastón planning to get money for Mía; she confronts Gastón demanding he tell her the truth, when he refuses she ends their relationship.

programme.title: Capítulo 182
programme.desc: Gastón tells Mía about himself and his past. Franco catches Mía and Gastón together. Gastón declares his love for Mía and asks Franco for a chance to prove he is worthy. Renata has suspicions about Madariaga. Franco and Miguel discuss Gastón. Alma takes Roberta far away to recover. Giovanni is allowed to return to school; he introduces his real parents to the class.

programme.title: Capítulo 183
programme.desc: Giovanni openly accepts his parents and his love for Vico in front of the entire school. Roberta wrecks on Simon's motorcycle. Miguel decides to finish with his revenge then return to Monterrey. Franco gets injured and Alma leaves him by himself. Mía has dance rehearsals; Gastón gets angry when she dances with Miguel. Tomás decides to stay with Pilar to get good grades. Gastón punishes Miguel.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Capítulo 185
programme.desc: Gastón punishes the students. Roberta devises a long con to get revenge on Diego. Valeria throws a fit when she learns that Mía will get her party; Franco admonishes her. Mía promises Gastón she'll stay away from Miguel. Franco begs Miguel for help with protecting Mía. Lupita tells Galia who stole the tests. Gandia withdraws his helping hand to Tomás. Gastón begins an investigation into Miguel. Pilar attacks Lupita.

programme.title: Capítulo 186
programme.desc: Valeria accuses Alma of trying to take Franco. Celina teams up with Pilar to get revenge on Lupita. Simon snaps an unflattering picture of Diego, Giovanni, and Tomás; copies are made anonymously. Celina scratches out Lupita's name on Mía's guest list. Miguel follows Gastón to a bank where he exits handcuffed to a briefcase. Roberta beats up Pilar to defend Lupita and blames her for the photo. Miguel and Mía partner up in class; he tells her to ask Gastón about his morning.

programme.title: Capítulo 187
programme.desc: Mía asks Gastón where he went, he tells her but then reveals he has a plan to get rid of Miguel. Mendiola congratulates Gandia then suspends Pilar for a week. Pilar tells Celina they have to make Lupita leave. Simon and Diego fight. Miguel updates Franco in return he put him in charge of Mía's party details. Lupita catches Celina vomiting. Gastón continues his investigation into Miguel. When Diego finds out Roberta is dating Simon, he tells her he loves her and kisses her. Madariaga continues to push Hilda and Hilario together. Gastón decides to leave for "Monterrey. Mía finds the letter to Lupita.

programme.title: Diva en el edificio
programme.desc: Los vecinos compiten en una trivia de televisión para que Lucía Méndez pase una tarde con ellos. Además, Silvia y Luis descubren que posiblemente esperen otro hijo.

programme.title: El sueño de Rocko
programme.desc: Rocko sueña con tener un grupo de rock llamado Los Moridos y empieza reunir a otros integrantes. Mientras, Jorjais decide mejorar su carrito para incrementar sus propinas.

programme.title: Basura en la escalera
programme.desc: Uno de los vecinos deja su basura en las escaleras, por lo que Luisito quiere encontrar al culpable. Además, Silvia hace de todo para ayudar a su sobrina a buscar empleo.

programme.title: La reina del Pik Pok
programme.desc: Erika Buenfil acude a la colonia para encontrar a nuevos talentos, pero termina conociendo a los vecinos. Además, don Hipólito tiene un nuevo trabajo para Germán.

programme.title: Cumpleaños de Luisito Junior
programme.desc: Es el cumpleaños de Luisito Jr. y Silvia se dispone a realizar una fiesta en privado. Sin embargo, los vecinos descubren el festejo y arman una celebración en grande.

programme.title: Bonita familia
programme.desc: Pedro pierde a Rambo en el póker con El Pechugo y Cris intenta recuperarlo. La familia Rivers recibe una mala noticia que tiene que ver con Don Lorenzo.

programme.title: Elote el guajolote
programme.desc: Vanesa recibe la visita de un inspector de seguridad en su clínica para cerciorarse de que todo esté en orden. Además, los vecinos aconsejan a Germán para conquistar a Virginia.

programme.title: Nos cayeron los chahuiztles
programme.desc: Casimiro Pérez, el hermano de Magda llega de visita. Además, la madrastra de Lorena le trae una noticia que cambiará su situación económica.

programme.title: Adopción
programme.desc: Cris shows Pedro the adoption application but they will receive a visit from the social worker. Germán, on the other hand, will be pruning the trees outside the building, without imagining the problems that this would bring.

programme.title: Buzón de quejas
programme.desc: Los problemas en el edificio revivieron un buzón de quejas que ya estaba olvidado, pero este buzón traerá problemas a Luis y Silvia. Por otro lado Morris esta a punto de reprobar el año.

programme.title: Can guash
programme.desc: Pedro se aterra por la llegada de su suegro pues tiene miedo de que no se lleven bien, además Germán y Vanessa emprenden un nuevo negocio.

programme.title: Oficinas de gobierno
programme.desc: Los vecinos aprovechan la apertura de unas nuevas oficinas de gobierno para emprender sus negocios y obtener algo de dinero, pero cuando Luis los descubre, intentará correrlos de la entrada del edificio.

programme.title: Llegada de Martina
programme.desc: Cris y Pablo están esperando a su hija Martina y, nerviosos por conocerla, preparan todo para recibirla. Por otro lado, Liz y Vanesa abren su tienda de ropa virtual y logran hacer un trato con Don Arturo.

programme.title: El gorrión de la canción
programme.desc: Dulce arrives at the building looking for the author of some songs to compose a new single for her. Also, Luis and Silvia meet the parents of one of their son's classmates.

programme.title: Alejandra godín
programme.desc: Arturo llega con la noticia de que Alejandra será una nueva empleada en la empresa donde trabaja. Por otro lado, una pequeña apuesta entre Germán y Birote crece sin control.

programme.title: Subasta
programme.desc: Magdalena se da cuenta que puede sacarle dinero a los objetos viejos de Arturo y se da a la tarea de hacer una subasta, carísima por cierto.

programme.title: Frankie publicista
programme.desc: Vanesa desesperada por salir de sus deudas, busca la ayuda de Frankie para diseñar la publicidad de su negocio, pero al final se da cuenta que el de las ideas realmente era otro.

programme.title: Oriundas
programme.desc: Magdalena receives an unexpected visit, a visit that will expose her past in Angangueo. Alejandra and Rocko try at all costs to take advantage of Magda's friend, who has always been in love with Arturo.

programme.title: Huele a muerto
programme.desc: Los vecinos están preocupados porque en el edificio hay un olor a descompuesto que provoca que todos sean sospechosos. Frankie y familia temen que la desaparición de Rocko esté ligada a que los López Pérez están comiendo carne.

programme.title: El carro de Don Arturo
programme.desc: Luis se ha vuelto obsesivo con la limpieza y Silvia intentará hacer algo para que se le quite la obsesión. Además, los López Pérez se han ganado un carro de lujo y lo presumirán a todos los vecinos.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: MeatEater - Sonoran Super-Birds: Mexico Gould's Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella crosses the border into Mexico's desert to call in the Gould's wild turkey. Of the five turkey subspecies in North America, the Gould's is the largest but rarest. On this unique Mexican adventure, Steve finds exciting action for gobbling toms. With locally made charcoal at his disposal, he'll grill up a big, brined, delicious bird.

programme.title: MeatEater - Straight Flush: Montana Mountain Grouse
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his friend Ronny Boehme drive into the mountains of southwest Montana to hunt the high ridge tops for dusky grouse. Ronny's Bracco Italiano hunting dogs have been bred and trained for this work, but can Ronny handle the altitude? The duo prepares dusky grouse jalape√±o poppers and grouse fettuccine with a white wine and cream sauce.

programme.title: MeatEater - Meet the Meateaters: Montana Crew Muley Part 1
programme.desc: The goal on MeatEater has always been to make viewers feel like they're on a hunt with Steven Rinella, but on this two-part episode Rinella grants his viewers an all-access pass to see just how the MeatEater crew works together to make the show. Strap-in for some behind-the-scenes action.

programme.title: MeatEater - Meet the Meateaters: Montana Crew Muley Part 2
programme.desc: The goal on MeatEater has always been to make viewers feel like theyre on a hunt with Steven Rinella, but.

programme.title: MeatEater - First Timers: Montana Mule Deer Part 1
programme.desc: Television personality Joe Rogan and his best friend, comedian Bryan Callen, join Steven Rinella for their very first hunt. On a float trip for mule deer through the rugged Missouri Breaks, this is as adventurous of a first hunt as anyone could hope for, and Steve's special guests are in for a memorable experience and delicious wild game field cooking in some gorgeous country.

programme.title: MeatEater - First Timers: Montana Mule Deer Part 2
programme.desc: Television personality Joe Rogan and his best friend, comedian Bryan Callen, join Steven Rinella for their very first hunt. On a float trip for mule deer through the rugged Missouri Breaks, this is as adventurous of a first hunt as anyone could hope for, and Steve's special guests are in for a memorable experience and delicious wild game field cooking in some gorgeous country.

programme.title: MeatEater - Swamp Stew: Michigan Bowfishing
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to his cherished boyhood stomping grounds along Michigans Muskegon River.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Wide Open: New Mexico Antelope
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and friend Cody Lujan head to Northeastern New Mexico to hunt pronghorn antelope, the fastest big game animal in the United States. In the wide open desert plains, Steve will have to work hard to stalk within range of a mature buck. The payoff will be antelope loin topped with local New Mexico chiles.

programme.title: MeatEater - Primal Cuts: Bone-in Cooking Special
programme.desc: In this cooking special, Steven Rinella shows his fans some of his favorite preparations for big, bone-in cuts of red meat. Interspersed with highlights from his recent caribou, buffalo, and mule deer hunts, the show features recipes that utilize some of the most underappreciated parts of big game animals. This episode is an attractive and delicious tribute to Steve's gun-to-table ethic.

programme.title: MeatEater - El Venado Blanco: Mexico Coues Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to his favorite hunting grounds of Sonora, Mexico to hunt for a Coues deer buck during the peak rutting season. Steve sets up camp and spends days glassing mountains and canyons for these elusive, wary desert whitetails. Meanwhile, his hunting partner, Remi Warren, attempts the difficult task of stalking one with a bow. To cap off the hunt, Steve cooks a batch of venison ribs braised in a Dutch oven buried beneath hot coals.

programme.title: MeatEater - Clash of the Titans: Northern California Pig & Deer
programme.desc: In this episode, Steven Rinella joins forces with fellow hunter and wild game chef Hank Shaw in the hills of Central California to go after Columbia blacktail deer, wild pigs, and a handful of small game species that are in season. The end result is a culinary smorgasbord of epic proportions.

programme.title: MeatEater - Sea Bear: Alaska Spring Bear and Seafood
programme.desc: Steve ventures out to his hunting and fishing shack on a remote coastline of southeast Alaska's Prince of Wales Island. While hunting bears from a skiff and a canoe, Steve gathers a variety of prime seafood and makes a discovery about his own motivations as a bear hunter.

programme.title: MeatEater - Full Boar: Whole Hog Cooking Special
programme.desc: Steven Rinella breaks down an entire boar and shares some of his favorite recipes in this Cooking Special. Matt Weingarter helps steve make head cheese, pork skin cracklins, smoked pork butt, and a tied-roast boned-out ham. Each dish requires thoughtful butchering, processing, and cooking to execute to perfection.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bull by Boat: British Columbia Moose
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan float a remote river on a bull moose hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Local Motion: Montana Bull Elk
programme.desc: Steve must deal with bad weather and fierce competition during a late-season central Montana elk hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Northern Rockies: British Columbia Grizzly Part 1
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan, a British Columbia guide, hike into the steep backcountry of northern British Columbia in search of grizzlies and black bears. Among the beutiful alpine vista, this adventure includes some intense and up-close bear action ‚Äì almost too close.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Northern Rockies: British Columbia Grizzly Part 2
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ryan Callaghan, a British Columbia guide, hike into the steep backcountry of northern British Columbia in search of grizzlies and black bears. Among the beutiful alpine vista, this adventure includes some intense and up-close bear action ‚Äì almost too close.

programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren give comedians Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen their first taste.

programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his buddy Doug Duren hunt with Joe Rogan.

programme.title: Lionel Steps Out
programme.desc: Archie's niece has been visiting the Bunkers and having a great time. She's even made friends with Lionel and loves going out on the town with him. However, a picture taken of the two of them becomes the thing that clues Archie in on their blossoming relationship and he hits the roof, citing that people should stick to their own race.

programme.title: The Bunkers And The Swingers
programme.desc: Edith finds a magazine on the subway and reads an ad in the "swap" section. Not realizing what is actually being asked for, Edith assumes the couple is looking for friendship. Mike and Gloria, in a rush, try to explain what they really want. However, it isn't until the couple is actually standing in the Bunker's living room that Edith realizes, with a little help from Louise, that they are "swingers" and they want to swap with Archie and Edith.

programme.title: Mike's Appendix
programme.desc: Both Archie and Gloria come home in bad moods after a hard day's work. This leads to yet another argument over women's liberation. Meanwhile, Mike has been having a pain in the side and the doctor tells him that he needs to have an appendectomy. This leads Archie and Gloria's argument to the proficiency of women doctors. Mike would rather have a more expensive male doctor rather than a cheaper woman doctor.

programme.title: Edith Flips Her Wig
programme.desc: Edith comes home from shopping in a terrible state; it seems she had been arrested for shoplifting. Apparently, she had walked out of a store, wearing a wig, while trying to give bus directions to a woman. Edith becomes convinced that she has become a kleptomaniac, after finding that she also "stole" a necklace. Edith begins to obsess and Mike suggests that she see a psychiatrist, however, Edith has something in mind herself; she visits a priest.

programme.title: The Locket
programme.desc: Edith's antique locket is missing and Archie sees it as a way to collect from the insurance company to buy a new television set. Confident of getting the insurance money, Archie orders a new color set, only to have an insurance adjuster arrive at the house to question the claim.

programme.title: Mike Comes Into Money
programme.desc: Mike comes into some money when his Uncle Alex dies and leaves him $275. Archie tries to persuade Mike into giving the money to him for room and board, but Mike would rather give it to George McGovern's presidential campaign. This sparks a big feud, leading to a dinner separation: Gloria and Mike buy and cook their own food while Edith and Archie do the same.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding
programme.desc: Edith is in a celebratory mood for Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary. While sitting around the dinner table enjoying a special cake, the family recalls Mike and Gloria's wedding. They also recall how Mike's Uncle Casimir met Archie and how they butted heads over who would perform the ceremony.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding - Pt. Ii
programme.desc: The family continues to reminisce on Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary and they recall the actual day Mike and Gloria got married. As Archie and Mike's Uncle Casimir continue to butt heads, Edith must gather up strength to tell Gloria about 'the birds and the bees'.

programme.title: Edith's Winning Ticket
programme.desc: When Edith finds some old lottery tickets that she has totally forgotten about, Archie hits the roof claiming she was wasting money. However, Archie's attitude quickly changes when the family discovers that one of the tickets is a winner, but there is a catch: Edith claims the tickets don't belong to her, but belong to George Jefferson.

programme.title: Archie Is Branded
programme.desc: One morning Archie gets up, goes to his front door to get the newspaper and is shocked to discover that someone has painted a swastika on his front door. Archie soon gets into a debate with Mike as to who could have painted it and why. As Archie is calling the police to report it, the Bunker home gets a visit from a man from a Hebrew vigilante group who wants to plan some retaliation.

programme.title: Archie And The Bowling Team
programme.desc: Archie comes home in a fantastic mood for a change and it's all because he has the chance to possibly join a prestigious bowling team, the Cannonballers. After waiting six years, it seems that there is finally a vacancy on the team and Archie is planning to try out, even though the team practices discrimination in admitting members.

programme.title: Archie In The Hospital
programme.desc: Archie has been suffering from a terrible pain in his back and has become a terrible pain to Edith, who has been waiting on him hand and foot. When the doctor takes a look at him, he orders Archie to be admitted to the hospital for tests. This is where Archie befriends the other patient in his room, but because of a partition, Archie doesn't realize the man is black.

programme.title: Archie And The Editorial
programme.desc: Archie gets fed up with a television editorial when this week's issue concerns gun control. Archie rails against gun control, battles it out with Mike and ultimately decides to go on the TV show with a rebuttal. Later, Archie meets his two biggest supporters -a pair of stickup artist who rob him at gunpoint.

programme.title: Archie's Fraud
programme.desc: Archie's Sunday job, driving taxi cabs for Bert Munson, has been a fine extra job for additional income. However, Archie has failed to report the extra income on his tax returns. Archie goes down to the IRS to explain, and soon discovers that he will be audited by the IRS. Trying to get out of it, Archie considers bribing the agent.

programme.title: Gloria And The Riddle
programme.desc: Gloria has become friends with a girl named Tammy and they have been organizing a Women's Fair. Male chauvinism is put to the test when Gloria poses a riddle to Mike and Archie who are stumped when they can't come up with the answer. Archie even goes to his drinking buddies for help, but to no avail. However, it is Edith who suddenly comes up with the answer (an answer that Archie doesn't want to hear).

programme.title: The Threat
programme.desc: Archie gets word that an old Army buddy who once saved his life is coming to town with his wife, Bobbi Jo. Archie offers to have them stay at his place. When Bobbi Jo arrives early, Archie gets an eyeful of the voluptuous woman and his guilt over his lust begins to show through to Mike and Gloria. Edith overhears a conversation between Archie, Mike and Gloria and out of concern for her marriage, makes a pivotal decision.

programme.title: Lionel Steps Out
programme.desc: Archie's niece has been visiting the Bunkers and having a great time. She's even made friends with Lionel and loves going out on the town with him. However, a picture taken of the two of them becomes the thing that clues Archie in on their blossoming relationship and he hits the roof, citing that people should stick to their own race.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Nancy Mace: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 24th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the chaos muppet herself, Nancy Mace. From her freaky obsession with trans people to her desperate attempts to kiss Trump's a**, is Nancy our most attention-seeking guy yet?

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Ice Goons at the Airport and DACA Detentions (w/ Jonathan V. Last))
programme.desc: Leave it to travel delays during Spring Break to turn America’s focus to immigration again. This week, as Congress debates over funding and reforms to ICE, and Markwayne Mullin is sworn in as new Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, Alex brings our attention back to the ongoing tactics the Trump administration is using to target immigrants and tear families apart. First she speaks to Stephanie Villarreal, whose husband has been detained despite being a DACA recipient. Then Alex speaks to Jonathan V. Last, editor of The Bulwark, about how to keep a spotlight on immigration, especially when they employ bureaucratic tactics to change someone's status, and why Trump is trying to force a voting rights bill into the debate.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (Paul Feig Reveals If He'll Make a Bridesmaids Sequel)
programme.desc: Paul Feig pours us a stiff one (or three) to talk erotic thrillers, political affairs, and Sydney Sweeney.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (Look on Trump's works, ye Mighty, and despair!)
programme.desc: The president makes waves with his Reflecting Pool reno, the U.S. treads water on the Iran peace process, and Trump’s crypto golden calf is more than a little fishy. This week, rock legend Melissa Etheridge lets the lesbian good times roll. Speaking of roll, Bosco brings the magic while we rank the sexy monsters of Magic: The Gathering. And to close us out, Lovett serves up a piping hot round of Second Thoughts.

programme.title: What a Day (Stacey Abrams On Why We Still Need The Voting Rights Act)
programme.desc:
The Supreme Court’s ruling in Louisiana v. Callais last month dismantled a critical portion of the Voting Rights Act. And since then, a slew of states — largely in the South — have moved to redraw voting maps. In doing so, they’re eliminating majority-Black voting districts. There are not many people who have done more to fight for Black voting power and for the rights of Black voters in the South than Stacey Abrams, host of Crooked Media’s Assembly Required. So we talked to her about the racist implications of Louisiana vs. Callais and where we go from here.
And in headlines, Senate Republicans once again block legislation that would halt President Donald Trump’s war of choice with Iran, Vice President JD Vance takes more steps in the administration’s anti-fraud initiative, and a Politico analysis finds that the Trump administration has lost in court more than 10,000 times in ICE detention decisions.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (GOP Gerrymanders Are Burning Down the House)
programme.desc: Redistricting battles are spreading like wildfire in both red and blue states, and increasingly the fate of America’s electoral system is at the mercy of the courts. This week, Alex speaks to Virginia Representative Suhas Subramanyam about how Democrats in the state are planning to fight fire with fire after a major setback for their efforts to redraw voting lines, and she hears from New York State Senator Mark Gianaris about the risks of diluting minority and progressive votes. Then Alex speaks to Stacey Abrams, host of Crooked Media’s Assembly Required, about the Supreme Court’s gutting of the Voting Rights Act and why Democrats can’t abandon their values to pursue Congressional seats.

programme.title: Hysteria (Runaway Virus)
programme.desc: Erin and guest host Alex Wagner (Runaway Country) cover the rapidly evolving Hantavirus outbreak situation, Trump’s latest pro-natalist initiative, and Republicans’ increasingly shameless plays to turn their careers in public service into careers as reality TV stars. They also react to Representative Virginia Foxx’s insane response to one of her child constituents, Alex points out Trump’s suspicious napping habit, and Erin squashes her beef with Blippi.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (We Bought a Ballroom)
programme.desc: Trump gives us a billion reasons to hate his ballroom, Kash Patel is drowning in leaks, and RFK Jr. will have to pry our pills from our cold, tan hands. Then Beck Bennett and Kyle Mooney tackle life's big questions and predict the next great Saturday Night Live host. And Lovett is on one when we take a look at our Second Thoughts.

programme.title: Nobody But A Frog Knows How To Live
programme.desc: A frog turned into a man wants Samantha to change him back.

programme.title: There's Gold In Them Thar Pills
programme.desc: Endora's doctor cures Darrin's cold, but produces strange side effects.

programme.title: Long Live The Queen
programme.desc: The Queen of the Witches abdicates and appoints Samantha to succeed her.

programme.title: Toys In Babeland
programme.desc: Endora brings a toy soldier to life to babysit with Tabitha.

programme.title: New Man In Town
programme.desc: Hazel and Rosie fall all over themselves, competing for the affections of a handsome Latin chauffeur (Robert Lowery), and ignoring other suitors.

programme.title: Herbert For Hire
programme.desc: The Johnson's are having some financial problems. Hazel refuses to make any more brownies for Mr. Griffin until he gives Mr. Johnson a job, despite the latter's lone credential being his degree in ancient languages.

programme.title: Hazel And The Lovebirds
programme.desc: Deirdre allows her daughter Nancy to move back from private school on the condition that she date someone other than Hazel's nephew, Eddy. Susan Silo appears as Gabriella, the Johnson's new maid. Robert Hogan appears as Bud, a gas station attendant that Deirdre also won't let Nancy date.

programme.title: Top Secret
programme.desc: Hazel takes Stan's daughter to meet a visiting senator (Larry Gates), but the girl inadvertently walks out with highly classified documents.

programme.title: The Sunshine Girls Quartette
programme.desc: Hazel, Rosie (Maudie Prickett) and a pair of Sunshine Girls team with Mitch (Dub Taylor) to form a musical group. However, a surprise birthday party for Mr. Griffin prevents Hazel from attending an audition in front of a traveling talent scout (Jean Willes).

programme.title: A Good Example For Harold
programme.desc: George uses a white lie to get out of meeting with an insurance agent. When Hazel catches Harold using the same type of white lie, she says everyone has to stop using lies. Hazel then has to tell Mr. Boyle (Philip Ober), the creator of cleaning product and a potential client for George, that the product doesn't work.

programme.title: Hazel's Highland Fling
programme.desc: Hazel attempts to stop Mr. Griffin from continually inviting himself for dinner because his new Scottish cook Angie is bad. She came to America to find her longtime boyfriend Gordie (James Doohan) and Hazel helps reunite the two of them.

programme.title: Ain't Walter Nice?
programme.desc: When Hazel's nephew Walter (Frank Aletter), a self-described promoter, arrives with hopes of obtaining a large investment from Mr. Griffin, George and Dorothy begin to question his motives.

programme.title: Mr. Griffin Throws A Wedding
programme.desc: Mr. Griffin takes it upon himself to plan the wedding of his nephew Pete (Dick Sargent) and George's stenographer, Maggie. When he also buys them a house and tries to find a new profession for Pete, Hazel steps in.

programme.title: Hazel And The Stockholders' Meeting
programme.desc: After Hazel's new Davidson vacuum cleaner stops working almost immediately, she takes it upon herself to attend the company's stockholder meeting to see that justice is done. Max Showalter appears as a member of the Davidson company. Byron Foulger appears as Mr. Zimmerman, the vacuum repair man.

programme.title: Hazel's Day Off
programme.desc: Busy Mr. Arden (James Westerfield) will only donate land for a new playground if the contract is drawn up on a Sunday afternoon. When he arrives, Hazel speaks her mind. William Schallert makes an appearance as Arden's attorney.

programme.title: I Been Singing All My Life
programme.desc: George's sister Deirdre is planning a talent show to raise money for the local children's hospital. Hazel wants to be part of the show, but Deirdre refuses to audition her and her friends. Circumstances force Deirdre to change her mind.

programme.title: The Fire's Never Dead While The Ashes Are Red
programme.desc: After George's former law professor (Vaughn Taylor) writes a bestseller, Hazel finds out that a local bookstore owner (Lurene Tuttle) used to date him long ago and sets out to reunite them.

programme.title: License To Wed
programme.desc: Deirdre's daughter Nancy (Davey Davison) is dating Hazel's nephew Eddie and it looks like they plan to marry. Deirdre does not want this since she thinks Eddie is not good enough for her daughter. Hazel thinks they are still too young to marry and uses psychology to prevent it.

programme.title: Genie With The Light Brown Lamp
programme.desc: The Baxters lose their dog Smiley on a road trip. Hazel reads Harold a bedtime story about Aladdin and the Magic Lamp. The next day he sees a gravy boat in a store window and thinks it is a magic lamp. Great things begin to happen for the family and Harold thinks it is because of the lamp.

programme.title: The Natural Athlete
programme.desc: George becomes jealous that Harold idolizes Hazel's championship bowling streak, so George secretly takes bowling lessons under an assumed name. Bill Zuckert appears as a bowling instructor.

programme.title: New Man In Town
programme.desc: Hazel and Rosie fall all over themselves, competing for the affections of a handsome Latin chauffeur (Robert Lowery), and ignoring other suitors.

programme.desc: Clothed Laura undraped in a portrait.

programme.desc: Stooges are locked in with a lion.

programme.desc: The boys' father wants to marry.

programme.title: Scotched in Scotland
programme.desc: Boys trail burglars to Scottish castle.

programme.title: Back From the Front
programme.desc: The trio goes to sea.

programme.title: A Snitch in Time
programme.desc: While working as furniture makers, the Stooges catch crooks in a boarding house.

programme.title: Cookoo Cavaliers
programme.desc: The boys buy a beauty salon by mistake.

programme.title: Not Guilty
programme.desc: Someone has stolen $300 from Mr. Drucker's general store, and it looks like Eb is the culprit. How else could he have bought himself a new car that cost, you guessed it, exactly $300? But that is just the first twist of the many turns of this story.

programme.title: How To Succeed In TV Without Really Trying
programme.desc: Lisa awakes, saying she's dreamt that she must invest in copper. When Oliver turns that idea down, she seizes on the opportunity to invest in a ten-year-old genius's electric company. Twenty-eight dollars buys her a third of the business.

programme.title: Arnold, Boy Hero
programme.desc: Arnold, the Ziffels' television-loving pig, has taken on a paper route to save enough money for a new color set. But when bank robbers show up and threaten his dreams, and Lisa and Oliver are held captive in their own home, Arnold has to fight back.

programme.title: Flight To Nowhere
programme.desc: The farmers of Hooterville are headed to France! Or at least they are if they can get organized. As part of an agricultural exchange program, the farmers are offered a chance to join a chartered government flight to Europe.

programme.title: My Mother The Countess
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is delighted to learn that her Hungarian mother is planning a visit to their little farm. Much to Oliver's (Eddie Albert) disbelief and dismay, a rumor spreads all around Hooterville that Lisa's mother is a European countess.

programme.title: The Spring Festival
programme.desc: Love is in the air as the first day of spring arrives in Hooterville. And who better to lead the town in celebrating it than Lisa. She is resolved to show Oliver just what spring is all about and sets out to produce a Spring festival celebration.

programme.title: Our Son, The Barber
programme.desc: It's a hair-raising episode as Eb (Tom Lester) enrolls in a correspondence course to become a barber. Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Eddie Albert) give him the money to learn the trade but learning how to cut hair by mail has its glitches.

programme.title: Oliver's Jaded Past
programme.desc: For a lawyer, Oliver should be more careful about what he signs. Lisa reminds him that two years ago Oliver made her a promise to take her back to New York for a two-week vacation. And she has it in writing� on the bottom of the kitchen drawer.

programme.title: The Hungarian Curse
programme.desc: It's hard to know who has a curse over Oliver, but he sure has his hands full with Lisa and her fellow Hungarian Lazlo Broslav. Lazlo appears with a letter claiming he once saved the life of Lisa's uncle, and now her family must repay the debt.

programme.title: The Rutabaga Story
programme.desc: There's a problem in Hooterville: no farmer seems able to grow a cash crop on account of the quality of the soil. But as it turns out from Oliver's (Eddie Albert) soil and mineral research, the oft misunderstood rutabaga is an ideal choice.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 1)
programme.desc: Green Acres goes Hollywood in this first installment of a two-part season finale. When Arnold the pig appears in a play at the Hooterville Theater, Lisa (Eva Gabor) arranges for an old friend to give Arnold a chance on the big screen.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 2)
programme.desc: It's a swine-sational third season finale for Green Acres, as Oliver and Lisa chaperone Arnold, the Ziffel's talented pig, to Hollywood for his big screen test. There's only one problem, Lisa failed to mention that Arnold is not your normal actor.

programme.title: Guess Who's Not Going To The Luau
programme.desc: Hooterville is abuzz as the townsfolk enter a contest sponsored by Peterson's Poi Juice, with a trip to Hawaii as the top prize. But no one expects the winner to be Arnold, the Ziffels' talented pig. Maybe a pig might fly after all, to the islands!

programme.title: The Rummage Sale
programme.desc: A charity rummage sale asks for donated clothing. Being among the wealthier residents of Hooterville, the organizers look to the Douglases. It isn't long before Oliver has to stop Lisa from giving away his entire wardrobe!

programme.title: Eb's Romance
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Eb is quickly engaged to Loreli Appleby, whom he has only known for a day. Eb tries to impress Loreli's father, a farmer, but in typical fashion, he accidentally ruins his future father-in-law's cabbage crop.

programme.title: Hail To The Fire Chief
programme.desc: When Fire Chief Joe Carson is looking for a way to go to Miami, he hires Oliver as his assistant, hoping Oliver will pay for the trip. The plan backfires, no pun intended, when Oliver starts pointing out what a poor job the chief has been doing.

programme.title: The Candidate
programme.desc: The highways in Hooterville are in dire need of repair. Oliver is fed up and believes he can make a difference by complaining to his state representative, Ben Hanks. But Hanks' popularity proves greater than his sense of responsibility.

programme.desc: Cheerleaders relive times.

programme.desc: A Kansas plumber becomes a king.

programme.desc: Reno considers a new identity.

programme.desc: Bob paints a winding meadow stream.

programme.desc: Storm waves pummel a grassy shore.

programme.desc: A path meanders alongside a quiet river.

programme.desc: Steve Ross paints a splashing waterfall.

programme.desc: A pond stagnates in a dank, dark forest.

programme.desc: Bob paints a lavender and green forest scene.

programme.desc: Bob paints a simple nature scene.

programme.title: Double Oval Stream
programme.desc: Bob paints within a double oval.

programme.title: Enchanted Forest
programme.desc: Bob paints a forest on partially black canvas.

programme.title: Lurch And His Harpsicord
programme.desc: Lurch becomes inconsolable when Gomez donates the family antique harpsichord to a collector. Gomez and Morticia offer Lurch a balalaika and a set of drums. When this proves unsatisfactory, Gomez and Fester decide to build a new harpsichord.

programme.title: Morticia, The Breadwinner
programme.desc: When Morticia hears the stock market has crashed, she assumes that Gomez has been financially wiped out and rallies the family to help make money. Morticia gives lessons, Lurch and Fester run an escort service, and the kids run a poison drink stand.

programme.title: The Addams Family And The Spaceman
programme.desc: While enjoying a moonlight picnic and snail hunt, the Addams Family is mistaken for Martians by uniformed investigators from Mysterious Space Objects Headquarters. In turn, Gomez (John Astin) and the clan regard their inquisitors with suspicion.

programme.title: My Son, The Chimp
programme.desc: An organ grinder's fugitive monkey leads Fester to believe that he has turned Pugsley into a monkey. Morticia and Gomez treat the monkey as though he were their son, welcoming him to the dinner table and giving him Pugsley's chores.

programme.title: Morticia's Favorite Charity
programme.desc: Moriticia's favorite charity is having an auction--so Morticia persuades her family to part with things to sell. Donations include Wednesday's headless doll, the old flogging table, a stuffed vulture and Uncle Fester's dynamite.

programme.title: Progress And The Addams Family
programme.desc: The Addams Family ignores notices that their house is to be condemned to make way for a freeway. When sudden explosions cannot be traced to any member of the family but, instead, to city workmen, Gomez is forced to consider moving the house.

programme.title: Uncle Fester's Toupee
programme.desc: Uncle Fester's pen pal, Madelyn, is coming to visit. Fester panics, as he has described himself as handsome, heroic and romantic. Gomez and Morticia agree that all Fester needs is a toupee and they help him make several styles.

programme.title: Cousin Itt And The Vocational Counselor
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez are worried about Cousin Itt because he spends his days sleeping and dreaming in his tiny room. They decide that Itt would make an excellent marriage counselor, and pretend to be a couple with a failing marriage.

programme.title: Lurch, The Teenage Idol
programme.desc: A recording company's platter of Lurch's gravelly voice brings a screaming mob of teenage fans to the Addams house. To calm the crowd, Lurch seats himself at the harpsichord and rewards his fans with a song to end all songs.

programme.title: The Winning Of Morticia Addams
programme.desc: Taken in by a psychologist's pronouncement that the only happily married couples are those who fight, members of the Addams household devise schemes to start a battle between Gomez (John Astin) and Morticia (Carolyn Jones).

programme.title: My Fair Cousin Itt
programme.desc: Gomez decides to bolster Cousin Itt's failing ego by making him the star of a production aimed as a birthday surprise for Wednesday. He hires the great director, Eric von Bissell, who gives Itt elocution lessons, causing a Hollywood star complex.

programme.title: Morticia's Romance (Part 1)
programme.desc: While Morticia and Gomez are celebrating their 13th wedding anniversary, she tells Wednesday and Pugsley a bedtime story of how she and Gomez first met. In this episode, Carolyn Jones plays Morticia, Morticia as a 22-year-old, and sister Ophelia.

programme.title: Morticia's Romance (Part 2)
programme.desc: Wednesday and Pugsley refuse to go to bed until they hear the rest of the courtship story. Morticia resumes the tale via flashback, including the ceremony beginning with Ophelia Frump, Morticia's balmy sister, as the bride.

programme.title: Morticia Meets Royalty
programme.desc: Romance seems close for Thing, the Addams right-hand-in-a-box, when Gomez's aunt arrives for a visit accompanied by Lady Fingers, her handmaiden. Thing enjoys the visit most because it gives him a chance to hold hands with one of his own kind.

programme.title: Gomez, The People's Choice
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez visit Mayor Henson to complain that their property tax is ridiculously low. After the Mayor proves his incompetence by offering a refund, Gomez is persuaded to become a candidate for the office.

programme.title: Cousin Itt's Problem
programme.desc: During a welcome home party for Cousin Itt, Morticia notices that the diminutive guest of honor seems to be losing his hair. Uncle Fester experiments with his chemistry set to produce a concoction that grows hair and tries the remedy on Itt.

programme.title: Halloween, Addams Style
programme.desc: Wednesday and Pugsley return in tears from trick-or-treating because a neighbor has told them there is no such thing as a witch. Morticia and Gomez plan to prove to the children that they do exist--by summoning a witch to a séance.

programme.title: How To Find Direction When Nothing Feels Right
programme.desc: From 6× Mr. Olympia to full-time business owner and stay-at-home dad, it’s safe to say Chris Bumstead’s been enjoying retired life. So what’s he been up to this past year, what’s changed most for him, and what’s he looking forward to next? Expect to learn how retirement is going for Cbum, if Cbum would have retired last year even if he didn’t win, how Chris learned to sit and process his emotions, if Cbum regrets retirement and if he misses the competing yet, how Chris plans on leveling up for baby Bumstead number two, he hardest part of losing that “Mr. Olympia structure” in daily life, if Chris ever plans on returning the stage again, and much more…

programme.title: Comforting Truths About Human Nature
programme.desc: How can we truly understand ourselves? Most of us either ignore our emotions or overthink them, turning simple feelings into complex puzzles. So how do we navigate the minefield of emotion to grow and become better? Expect to learn where self-esteem really comes from, why criticism hurts so much, where imposter syndrome comes from and how to overcome it, if hustle culture is a modern phenomenon or is this something new, why we tend to intellectualise our emotions, why we have existential crisis’ and how to navigate them, why online dating is so miserable for so many people and much more…

programme.title: How to Treat Men Better
programme.desc: Are modern gender dynamics leaving men feeling ‘castrated’? While men are often blamed for today’s social problems, far less attention is given to how shifting expectations of women and relationships may also shape male identity. What role do women play in this dynamic—and how can it be improved? Expect to learn how important it is for women to seem impressed or giddy or appreciative of their partner, how women can appear more receptive or appreciative, why men are being emasculated by women, how women can stop castrating men and much more…

programme.title: How to Defeat Your Stress, Anxiety & Inaction
programme.desc: If motivation can’t be trusted, what should you rely on instead? Bestselling author and top podcaster Mel Robbins explains why discipline and emotional control are the real keys to success, and how to build the mindset needed to follow through when it counts. Expect to learn why we cling to control even when we know it makes us miserable and why we focus on things we can't control, how to better deal with the judgment of others, where self-compassion comes from, how to get a grip on your anxiety and ADHD, what ""Let Them"" theory means and how to employ it, where self-compassion actually comes from and much more...

programme.title: The Personal Pain of Country Music
programme.desc: Odds are, your favorite country song wasn’t written by the person singing it; it was probably written by HARDY (or someone in his writing circle, maybe). So how did a Mississippi country boy become one of Nashville’s most powerful behind-the-scenes writers and producers? Expect to learn what HARDY’s philosophy for his career and life is, what most people do not understand about how the Nashville scene works, how to balance the unique tension between being a songwriter and an artist, the story of the bus crash that almost killed HARDY, how to use vulnerability and turn it into a strength and much more…
